metrics.text: Revenue ~$12M GMV (internal estimate — Tony has not confirmed actuals). 300–400 orders/day including quotes and repair authorizations; 60% through website, 40% via phone/manual PO. 65–70% straight price book purchases, 30–35% custom quoting. 40,000 SKU-intensive catalog — market leader in fitness equipment parts space. Previous failed replatform cost $250K+. Soft launch target: EOY 2026. Full relaunch: early 2027.

economic_buyer.text: Vince Smith (Interim CEO / Chairman, PE firm) is the real economic buyer — has empowered Tony to 'just go' with no lengthy approval process. Tony Meirovitz (VP E-commerce) is DM for day-to-day decisions; oversees E-commerce, Marketing, and Customer Service. Mike Montoy (Head of Operations, 22+ years) is key operational stakeholder. All three attended May 20 discovery call.

decision_criteria.text: B2B + D2C on Shopify Plus. Jordan confirmed 80% OOB Shopify (meta fields + meta objects); 20% custom with DYODE. Custom modules: cable CPQ (fixed labor + component retail cost), electronics exchange and buyback program (separate pricing tier), repair return shipping labels (UPS API). Four-tier B2B pricing with volume discounts. Net 30 terms standard; credit card payments primary. Consolidated single shipping charge from two warehouses (LA electronics + Illinois general parts). Shopify native search first — upgrade to Algolia later only if needed (Tony has SearchSpring scar tissue). Flat file ERP bridge until NetSuite migration (before April 2027). AI/agentic commerce via Universal Commerce Protocol (auto-on with Shopify). AutoZone-style make/model/brand → parts filter with PDF line drawings and installation videos.

decision_process.text: DYODE (Tim Katz co-founder, Elana K. Director Ops & Product, Paul Sr. Dev/SA, Kevin Stites Director Web Dev) is confirmed implementation partner. May 20 discovery completed. Run Payments analysis circulated May 29 (Jordan + DYODE). Elana asked for pricing breakdown Jun 3. Mercedes proposed parallel contract review — Tony confirmed Vince moves fast with no lengthy approvals.

paper_process.text: Tony shared the paper process will be quick — Vince (PE/Interim CEO) empowered to move without lengthy approvals.

identified_pain.text: Legacy ERP (~20 years old) creates extreme site lag — 700+ individual API calls per part lookup. Current site not mobile-friendly (Google SEO penalties). Previous $250K replatform failed: not vetted, not tested, slammed to production — customers reverted immediately. Large B2B customers (24 Hour Fitness, LA Fitness) send 75–100 line item POs that CS agents manually key. Two warehouse locations cause double shipping charges and inability to mix cart items from both. Old failed site still being indexed by Google (Tony actioning).

champion.text: DYODE team (Tim Katz, Elana K., Paul SA, Kevin Stites) are strongest advocates — Tim: 'only group I would trust.' Tony Meirovitz (VP E-comm) is DM — 20+ years e-commerce experience, prior Shopify familiarity, DYODE ally. Vince Smith (Interim CEO/Chairman, PE) is executive sponsor who moves fast. Jordan Murphy (SE) confirmed 80% OOB Shopify fit on May 20 call.

competition.text: No direct competitors mentioned. Previous platform was a bespoke custom .NET site. Evaluating Shopify vs status quo rebuild. BigCommerce not raised. Tony has prior Shopify experience. Active topic: Shopify Payments vs Run Payments (Jordan's analysis circulated May 29).

national-gym-supply.strategy: Discovery complete — Tony confirmed Shopify direction, Vince (PE/Interim CEO) is economic buyer with mandate to move fast. DYODE (Paul + Kevin) are the confirmed build team. Jordan confirmed 80% OOB Shopify on May 20. Critical path: (1) close pricing breakdown for Elana (asked Jun 3), (2) lock Run vs Shopify Payments decision, (3) send contracts for parallel review, (4) scope custom modules (cable CPQ, electronics exchange). Stage now Deal Craft — moving to close.

risks.text: Custom module scope — cable CPQ, electronics exchange/buyback, repair return labels. Tony 'a little nervous' about customization. Must confirm app vs custom route with DYODE.
risks.mit: DYODE (Paul SA) to assess. Identify Shopify app options for CPQ and exchange programs before scoping custom dev.

risks.text: Revenue/GMV not confirmed on May 20 call — Tony hasn't shared actuals. $12M is internal estimate. Affects Shopify Plus pricing conversation.
risks.mit: Ask Tony directly for 2025 revenue numbers on next call. Roll into Elana pricing-breakdown response.

risks.text: Jun 30 close date with stage Deal Craft — paper not yet circulating.
risks.mit: Send contracts for parallel review this week. Vince empowered to move fast — use it.

risks.text: Search is Tony's top priority — SearchSpring scar tissue means bar is high. Shopify native search must be validated before commit.
risks.mit: Demo Shopify search and discovery on broader team call. Confirm serial number and part number fuzzy matching meets requirements.

Key attendees May 20: Tony Meirovitz (tmeirovitz@gympart.com), Mike Montoy (mmontoy@gympart.com), Vince Smith (vsmith@gympart.com). manager_q.urgency: EOY 2026 soft launch — Q3 contract required. Vince empowered to move fast.

metrics.text: B2B GMV ~$5M global (confirmed by Nicole Apr 29). 90% B2B / 10% DTC. ~300 active B2B accounts globally. 7 Latin American origins (Mexico, Guatemala, Nicaragua, El Salvador, Colombia [largest], Peru, Ecuador). Destination offices: Houston (NA), Dublin & London (EU), Australia (APAC). Founded ~2001. Phase 1 scope: Spot coffee (~15% of inventory). Forward contracting deferred to phase 2.

economic_buyer.text: Mark Howard (Business & Customer Relations Head) likely economic influencer. Nicole Freydell (Brand Business Leader and Ecom Mgr) is champion candidate. Luz Carime Gomez Cocoma (IT Director, London) owns NetSuite + Roaster Hub stack and joined Jun 2 call.

decision_criteria.text: Customer approval workflow, sample-first flow, 2–3 sample limit (now with a fee), payment at warehouse release, NetSuite stays SoT for inventory/pricing/payment. Phase 1: Spot coffee only. PIM path open (NetSuite ↔ Atlas vs direct Shopify ↔ Atlas) — Nicole verifying. Forward contract workflow: 3-year contracts, pre-shipment sample approval, invoice when coffee lands in warehouse — phase 2. Pricing complexity: NY C-market daily + origin differentials + variety value + certifications.

decision_process.text: Apr 29 intro → May 26 technical discovery → Jun 2 technical discovery (Mercedes, Jordan Murphy SE, Nicole, Luz attended). Next: B2B + NetSuite integration demo (Jordan + Mercedes). Mercedes to send scheduling email for next week / week after, AM ET for Luz's London tz. Close date 2026-08-31 → SF shows 2026-09-30.

paper_process.text: Not yet mapped — close on next call sequence.

identified_pain.text: Migrating from custom React Roaster Hub on top of NetSuite (not on SuiteCommerce). 90% of business is wholesale and almost none of it is digitized — customers ordering manually. Spot offering is the wedge — micro-roaster self-serve in NA market (~10K new micro-roasters/year per Nicole). Phased rollout: spot first, complex forward contracts in phase 2 — but architect once.

champion.text: Nicole Freydell Restrepo (Brand Business Leader and Ecom Mgr) is engaged and driving the evaluation. Luz Carime Gomez Cocoma (IT Director, London) now in the loop and owns the integration stack. Mark Howard likely economic influencer.

competition.text: Status quo (custom React Roaster Hub) is the primary competition. Two DTC Shopify Plus projects already running internally (Kasalandino + James Hoffmann fermentation) — proves Shopify familiarity. Planned 'Marcado' US DTC launch.

caravela-coffee.strategy: Jun 2 technical discovery made real progress — decisions locked on customer approval workflow, sample-first flow, sample fees, warehouse-release payment, NetSuite as SoT. Phase 1 scope (Spot, ~15% of inventory) is now agreed. Critical path: (1) Mercedes sends scheduling email for B2B + NetSuite integration demo (Jordan + Mercedes), (2) Luz verifies PIM path NetSuite↔Atlas vs Shopify↔Atlas, (3) close paper process gap. MEDDPICC moved from 2/16 (May 13) to 9/16 (May 13 update — pre-Jun 2) — likely 10–11/16 post Jun 2.

risks.text: Paper process and procurement path still not mapped
risks.mit: Surface on next demo call. Ask Nicole/Luz directly: who signs?

risks.text: PIM path undecided — NetSuite↔Atlas vs Shopify↔Atlas
risks.mit: Luz to verify before integration demo.

risks.text: Multi-region complexity (Houston, Dublin, London, Australia) — VAT, multi-currency, tz
risks.mit: Architect once now even though phase 1 is Spot-only NA.

risks.text: Forward contracting deferred to phase 2 — risk that priorities shift before phase 1 ROI proven
risks.mit: Phase 1 deliberately tight; deliver spot revenue capture quickly.

metrics.text: PBR $165K. Business revenue, order volume, and SKU count not yet captured in discovery. ROI case not yet built — integration replacement (Salesforce + Sage) is the stated driver.

economic_buyer.text: Not yet identified. Kyle Boyd (kyle@artificialturfsupply.com) is primary contact but title and budget authority unknown. No executive sponsor mapped.

decision_criteria.text: Q4 go-live target (Oct 2026). Build starts July. Must integrate with Salesforce (CRM) and Sage (ERP/accounting) to replace legacy systems. Specific integration data flows not yet documented.

decision_process.text: Technical discovery completed May 5 with Kyle Boyd and Jordan Murphy (SE). Next meeting first week of July after build scope is confirmed. Formal eval process, competitive shortlist, and other stakeholders not yet mapped.

identified_pain.text: Legacy Salesforce + Sage integrations require replacement — current setup creating operational friction. Q4 switchover is a stated business target with build starting July, indicating real urgency to decide quickly.

champion.text: Kyle Boyd is engaged and actively moving the deal forward with Jordan Murphy (SE). Authority level not confirmed. Jordan Murphy is engaged as SE champion on the Shopify side.

SE Jordan Murphy is the key asset here — leverage the July meeting to surface economic buyer, build the ROI case, and nail down integration scope for Salesforce + Sage before the July build start. The May→July gap is 6+ weeks dark — stay in touch with Kyle to maintain momentum.

risks.mit: July meeting must surface EB and business case. Prep Jordan with EB discovery questions.

Integrations: Salesforce (CRM) + Sage (ERP/accounting) replacing legacy. Next meeting 1st week of July. Quiet this week — no new activity.

sf.name: Viaggio Pontoons-D2C & B2B - Split for B2B

metrics.text: ~$5M B2B GMV potential (internal estimate). 137 dealers, 5 territories, 7 sales reps. 4 brands: Viaggio Pontoons, Lakota Trailers, SMC, Duo Form (Bristol, Indiana). Current platform cost: NetSuite SuiteCommerce ~$35K/yr. Quote process: dealer selects options → submits → sales reps recreate in NetSuite (no deposit, fully manual). Target go-live August 2027 (model year 2028 launch).

economic_buyer.text: Owner Eric is likely final decision maker — attended Nathaniel's May 12 leadership pitch alongside CFO/Corporate Controller, Head of Sales, and Head of CS. All four stakeholders are now aware of the Shopify evaluation. Nathaniel (Director of IT) is day-to-day champion but lacks budget authority. Neither Mercedes nor Joe have direct contact with Eric or CFO yet.

decision_criteria.text: Unified Shopify B2B (dealer portal) + D2C (boat owner portal) on one platform. Dealer configurator with rule-based pricing logic. Multi-brand architecture must extend from Viaggio design to Lakota Trailers, SMC, Duo Form. Partner requirements: current tech standards, no IP lock-in, no AI buzzwords. Replace NetSuite SuiteCommerce. Proforma invoices for dealer financing. Two invoices per boat (boat + motor separately).

decision_process.text: Nathaniel pitched leadership week of May 12 — Owner, CFO, Head of Sales, Head of CS all attended. May 20 debrief — Mercedes' first call as B2B specialist. May 28 Technical Reconnect completed. Jun 1 partner intro calls with Zaelab + Domaine. Decision approaching mid-June. Stage advanced to Solution. Co-sell: Joe Hemingway is AE (SF opp owner), Mercedes is B2B specialist SE.

identified_pain.text: NetSuite SuiteCommerce ~$35K/yr explicitly not built for B2B (confirmed by NetSuite engineers themselves). Simple SuiteCommerce changes take 6 months via third-party devs. Nathaniel is a team of one. Quote workflow is fully manual — dealer selects options, submits, then sales reps recreate the entire order in NetSuite with no deposit. Two separate invoices per boat (boat + motor) creates friction. Proforma invoices required for dealer financing add complexity.

champion.text: Nathaniel Talbot (Director of IT, newly promoted) is the primary internal champion — built the 5-year cost analysis and pitched leadership internally himself. Joe Hemingway (AE) brought the deal. Mercedes Boykin-Sabalones is B2B specialist SE (co-sell). Strong internal conviction — Nathaniel has already done the internal selling. Jordan Murphy now looped in as B2B specialist SE.

competition.text: Status quo (NetSuite SuiteCommerce) is the primary competition. No other platforms mentioned. NetSuite engineers themselves acknowledged SuiteCommerce was not designed for B2B — strong position for Shopify B2B. Partner shortlist (Jun 1 calls): Zaelab + Domaine.

viaggio-pontoons.strategy: Stage advanced to Solution. SF close moved to 2026-07-31. Co-sell with Joe Hemingway (AE). Mercedes is B2B specialist SE. Champion Nathaniel built the internal business case himself. Critical path: (1) close out partner selection between Zaelab + Domaine after Jun 1 intro calls, (2) get Mercedes + Joe in front of Owner Eric and CFO before mid-June decision point, (3) complete RFI response review with Jordan, (4) drive paper process. Multi-brand expansion (Lakota, SMC, Duo Form) is the long-term prize.

risks.text: Decision deadline approaching mid-June — complex B2B + D2C platform architecture with multi-brand scope
risks.mit: Solution-stage work in flight: partner intros done Jun 1, RFI response with Jordan, get Eric/CFO meeting before mid-June.

risks.text: Economic buyer (Owner Eric, CFO) not yet in direct contact — Nathaniel is only intermediary. Deal could stall if Eric is not fully committed.
risks.mit: Joe + Mercedes to get on a call with Eric and CFO before mid-June decision. Nathaniel's internal pitch is strong but needs validation from Mercedes/Joe directly.

risks.text: Dealer configurator with rule-based pricing logic — complexity scoping still needed
risks.mit: Coordinate with selected partner (Zaelab or Domaine) post-Jun 1 calls.

risks.text: Paper process not yet mapped — close at risk for Jul 31 target
risks.mit: Surface procurement path on next call.

metrics.text: Revenue ~$14.1M. ~721 monthly visits (quote-driven, not browse-driven). 50 years in business. ~2,970 SKUs. Manufacturer's rep + distributor model — B2B only, territory-based sales. Top brands: Endress+Hauser (603), Dwyer (534), Sick (387), Burkert (381), ASCO (317), Ashcroft (176). West Jordan, UT HQ.

economic_buyer.text: Scott Johnston — VP of Systems & Marketing — single decision-maker at this stage, owns both Systems AND Marketing budgets. Champion candidate AND likely economic buyer for early stack consolidation conversation. Founder/CEO not yet engaged.

decision_criteria.text: Not yet fully surfaced. Initial angles: platform consolidation (Weebly marketing site + Yodify B2B catalog + line card subdomain → one), sales-rep productivity (Russell Hendrix analogue), capture self-serve demand currently quote-gated. ERP and CRM TBD — confirm on next call.

decision_process.text: Cold outbound by Grant Dugas (Shopify B2B Consultant) May 27. Scott Johnston accepted chat May 29; intro/discovery meeting Jun 2 (3:00–3:30pm ET, Google Meet). Cooper VandeWalle assigned SE — not yet looped in. No formal procurement path yet. First contact stage — pre-Opp until Jun 2.

identified_pain.text: Three separate properties (rustco.com Weebly, shop.rustco.com Yodify catalog, linecard.rustco.com) — fragmented stack. Quote-driven catalog with login-gated pricing means self-serve demand is leaking. 50-yr-old manufacturer's rep / distributor model in industrial automation — Russell Hendrix proves the analogue (5x faster order processing for reps, 24% revenue increase on Shopify Plus).

champion.text: Scott Johnston (VP Systems & Marketing) — accepted the cold outbound May 29, took the meeting Jun 2. Owns both Systems and Marketing — rare combined-budget profile that simplifies early evaluation. Grant Dugas (Shopify B2B Consultant) sourced and warmly handed off.

competition.text: Status quo: Weebly + Yodify + line card subdomain. Yodify is cheap — ROI framing must be revenue capture + rep productivity, not platform cost. No competing platforms surfaced yet.

rust-automation-and-controls.strategy: Pre-Opp first-contact deal — Jun 2 intro happened. Strategic angle: platform consolidation (Weebly + Yodify + line card → one) + sales-rep productivity (Russell Hendrix analogue) + capture self-serve demand currently quote-gated. Critical path: (1) loop in Cooper VandeWalle (assigned SE), (2) get next call on books for technical discovery, (3) confirm ERP/CRM stack, (4) start qualifying budget + buying committee around Scott. Watch out: Yodify is cheap — must lead with revenue capture, not cost.

risks.text: Pre-Opp — discovery just started, qualification is thin (4/16 MEDDPICC)
risks.mit: Run a tight technical discovery on next call. Surface ERP/CRM, decision committee, urgency.

risks.text: Yodify is cheap — Plus pricing conversation will compress if ROI framing isn't right
risks.mit: Lead with Russell Hendrix case study (5x rep productivity, 24% rev). Never frame as platform cost play.

risks.text: Cooper VandeWalle (assigned SE) not yet looped in
risks.mit: Brief Cooper before next call. Get on books together.

risks.text: ERP/CRM unknown — could create integration scoping surprises
risks.mit: Ask Scott directly on next call. Get him to walk the stack.

rust-automation-and-controls.notes: Industrial automation B2B distributor + manufacturer's rep, 50 yrs in business, ~$14.1M revenue, ~2,970 SKUs. Contact: Scott Johnston (VP Systems & Marketing, scott.johnston@rustco.com, 801-566-7878). SE: Cooper VandeWalle (assigned, not yet looped in). Sourced by Grant Dugas (Shopify B2B Consultant). Comparable wins: Russell Hendrix (Canada's largest foodservice equipment supplier, manufacturer's-rep model, 5x faster order processing for reps, 24% revenue increase, 43% B2B online order volume in 12 months); Fumex Inc. (industrial filtration). Watch out: Yodify is cheap — ROI framing must be revenue capture + rep productivity, not platform cost. Pricing: do NOT quote Plus pricing in early calls — Plus has VPF, verify via Vault before stating.

meddic.metrics: Ardmore Home Design (AHD) — founder-led luxury home décor company, founded 2008, HQ City of Industry CA with showrooms in NY, Dallas, LA, Atlanta. Wholesale/trade-only model selling to interior designers and boutique/luxury retailers globally. Portfolio of 5 brands: Made Goods (flagship furniture), Blue Pheasant (tabletop — the brand in scope), Pigeon & Poodle (bath/accessories), Thucassi (candles/fragrance), Burton James (upholstery, acquired 2024). Revenue / order volume not yet quantified — PBR unset pending discovery. Opp scope: Plus DTC + B2B.
meddic.economic_buyer: Not confirmed. Kenar Maronian (Director of Digital) is the primary contact and re-initiated the conversation. Founder/CEO Chris DeWitt + co-founder Oscar Yague; exec team incl. Shane Brogan (COO), Darci MacNab (CFO), Pamela Burke (Global Sales Director) — surfaced from the prior cycle, none confirmed as EB for this effort.
meddic.decision_criteria: Not yet surfaced — discovery Jun 19. Opp scope (Plus DTC + B2B) implies a B2B wholesale portal + DTC storefront for Blue Pheasant, with potential expansion to other AHD brands ("anything else on your radar"). Current platform(s) unknown.
meddic.decision_process: Re-engagement transferred from Joe Chung (Jun 8). Kenar reached back out to Joe Jun 5 wanting to revisit moving Blue Pheasant to Shopify. Reconnect/discovery call booked Fri Jun 19, 2pm PST. No formal eval process, committee, or timeline mapped yet.
meddic.pain: Hypothesis (unconfirmed): trade/wholesale ordering likely manual; multi-brand portfolio may sit on fragmented systems. Kenar's outreach signals active intent to move Blue Pheasant to Shopify. Why-now to be confirmed Jun 19.
meddic.champion: Kenar Maronian (Director of Digital, kmaronian@madegoods.com) — re-initiated contact and owns the digital mandate. Engaged but influence/budget authority unconfirmed. Strongest champion candidate.

ardmore-home-design.strategy: THIN / pre-discovery — re-engagement of an account with 3 prior Closed Lost cycles (2024–2025, all under Joe Chung). Kenar (Director of Digital) reached back out, so intent is warm. Jun 19 priorities: (1) understand what stalled the prior cycles and what changed, (2) map the multi-brand structure and confirm scope (Blue Pheasant first vs broader AHD), (3) surface EB + current platform + why-now, (4) gather enough to set a PBR. Treat Jun 19 as pure discovery; do not pitch.

risks.text: Account has 3 prior Closed Lost opps (2024–2025) — prior cycles repeatedly stalled.
risks.mit: Open discovery by surfacing what killed the earlier attempts and what is materially different now.

risks.text: THIN qualification — pre-discovery. EB, criteria, process, and pain all unconfirmed; PBR unset.
risks.mit: Run a structured discovery Jun 19; set PBR once volume/scope are known.

risks.text: Multi-brand portfolio (5 brands) — scope ambiguity between Blue Pheasant and broader AHD.
risks.mit: Clarify scope early: which brand(s) and which channels (DTC vs B2B) are in phase 1.

ardmore-home-design.notes: Ardmore Home Design (AHD), City of Industry CA — wholesale luxury home décor parent of Made Goods, Blue Pheasant, Pigeon & Poodle, Thucassi, Burton James. Opp "Blue Pheasant - Plus DTC + B2B" (Pre-Qualified, close 12/31/26, PBR unset). Primary contact: Kenar Maronian, Director of Digital (kmaronian@madegoods.com). Transferred from Joe Chung Jun 8; Kenar re-initiated Jun 5. 3 prior Closed Lost opps (Burton James Plus;Payments, Blue Pheasant Plus, Blue Pheasant ShopTalk). Reconnect call Fri Jun 19, 2pm PST. ardmorehomedesign.com.

qualifying_questions: What stalled the earlier Shopify conversations with Joe, and what's changed since?
Which brands and channels are in scope first — Blue Pheasant only, or broader AHD? DTC, B2B/wholesale, or both?
What platform(s) run the brands today, and what's the biggest friction?
Who owns the budget and final sign-off for this initiative?
What's driving the timing now, and is there an internal deadline?
